resources.string: Sign in with your own Twitch account to get started.
Use your own streamer account — you can add a bot account later from the dashboard.
Sign in with your Twitch account
Open Twitch and enter this code at twitch.tv/activate:
The code expired before you approved it. Try again.
The login was declined on Twitch.
By continuing you agree to the terms of service and privacy policy.
Sign in with your Twitch account to get started.
Link Spotify to enable song requests.
Link Discord for notifications and server sync.
Link YouTube to enable the YouTube music provider.
Enter your app credentials below — no config files needed. Finish each required step, then continue to sign in with Twitch.
Here's what you set up. Finish to sign in with your Twitch account.
Finish your required steps to continue.
Twitch sign-in failed. Please try again.
The bot talks to Twitch through your own Twitch app. Create one and paste its credentials.
Open the Twitch Developer Console at https://dev.twitch.tv/console/apps and register a new application.
Set the OAuth Redirect URL to exactly the address shown below.
Choose the "Chat Bot" category, create it, then copy the Client ID and generate a Client Secret.
Sign in as the Twitch account the bot posts chat from, and approve its chat permissions.
Log into Twitch as the bot's own account in this browser first.
Start the authorization, then approve the requested chat scopes.
Connect a Spotify app to enable song requests.
Add the Redirect URI shown below.
Copy the Client ID and Client Secret.
Connect a Discord app for notifications and guild sync.
Add the OAuth2 Redirect shown below.
Copy the Client ID and Client Secret from the OAuth2 page.
Connect a Google app to enable the YouTube music provider.
Add the Authorized redirect URI shown below.
Copy the Client ID and Client Secret.
From your Twitch app's settings page.
Generated on the Twitch app page — shown only once.
This workspace is being built — its tools land in an upcoming slice.
You're signed in as a viewer

This account has no management role on this channel, so there's no dashboard to show. You can still take part from chat and the channel's public pages. If you should have access, ask the streamer to add you.
Could not load the song queue: %1$s
Delete the timer "%1$s"? This cannot be undone.
Unban %1$s? They'll be able to chat in your channel again.
Remove %1$s from the queue? It won't be played.
Ban %1$s? They won't be able to chat until you lift it.
Lift the ban on %1$s? They'll be able to chat again.
Connect your bot account and the services you stream with.
The account your bot chats from.
YouTube music provider and stream stats.
Cross-post alerts and notifications to your server.
The bot needs %1$s to %2$s. Grant it in one click — your existing permissions are kept.
The bot needs %1$d more Twitch permission(s) to power some features. Grant them in one click — your existing permissions are kept.
Open Twitch and enter this code at twitch.tv/activate:
Waiting for you to approve on Twitch…
Authorize the bot account on Twitch
Sign in as the bot account, then enter this code at twitch.tv/activate:
Waiting for you to approve on Twitch…
Could not load your stream info: %1$s
Turning the economy off stops viewers from earning. You can turn it back on later.
Delete the alert for %1$s? This can't be undone.
OBS browser-source overlays for your stream.
Delete %1$s? Its browser-source URL will stop working.
Export your channel's full event ledger as a file you can back up or move to another deployment, or import a ledger you exported before. Importing only adds events that aren't already there.
This adds the file's events to your channel's ledger. Events already present are skipped, so it can't make duplicates, but it can't be undone. Continue?
The bot talks to Twitch through a Twitch app. Use the shared app or paste your own credentials.
Create a Twitch app and paste its credentials, or keep using the shared app.
Open the Twitch Developer Console at https://dev.twitch.tv/console/apps and register a new application.
Set the OAuth Redirect URL to exactly the address shown below.
Choose the "Chat Bot" category, create it, then copy the Client ID and generate a Client Secret.
Generated on the Twitch app page — shown only once.
Leave blank to keep the current secret.
Saving new credentials replaces the current ones. The bot may need to re-authenticate.
%1$s needs its own app credentials before you can connect. Create an app on the %1$s developer console and paste its Client ID and Secret.
No chat yet. Messages appear here as they arrive.
Send a message as the bot…
Delete this message from %1$s? This can't be undone.
Time out %1$s? They won't be able to chat for a while.
No quotes yet. Add the first one.
Delete quote #%1$d? This can't be undone.
Connect a Discord server from the Integrations page to post notifications.
Delete the %1$s notification rule? This can't be undone.
No managed members or permits yet.
Remove %1$s's management role? They'll lose this access.
Revoke %1$s's permit? They'll lose this access.
No pipelines yet. Create the first one.
No steps yet. Add the first one.
Search for a song to request…
Your account is frozen — spending is paused.
No games are available right now.
Pronouns are shown from your Twitch profile and can't be edited here yet.
